Cartoon Network airing an hour block of DBZ every weekday from 4:30 to 5:30 until season 3 ends.



Androids: Assassins and Androids: Invinicible now available in some stores.



Trunks DVDs ("Mysterious Youth" and "Prelude to Terror") expected late August to early September



Season 4 (Trunks and beyond) to begin airing September 4th on Cartoon Network.



Graphic novels of DB and DBZ coming out from Viz in August and September, corresponding with the Japanese volumes 1 and 17.



BK Promo VHS to be released mid-September in cut and uncut format.



FUNimation is looking for developers to make a DBZ game; however, nothing has been finalized yet



(Last Update: 08/19/2000)




 - B.A.D. Mini-Shrine
 - DBZ Cracked
 - Ginga GIRI GIRI

Should Viz Censor the Dragonball Comic?

Yes

Dragonball
Lycos 50 Rank: #3

Namek
Highest Day: 27044

Translate
powered by SYSTRAN Translation Software




Current Features: Costume Contest

Friday - 08/18/2000

Mr.E @ 23:33 pst - E-mail Poop...
Another update from Mr.E, or as I am better known, Captain Wacky! Yes, our very frightening and demented odd e-mails section has received an update. Ho boy, some of you are real sick puppies. Doubtless to say, your mind will become a useless pile of goo once you have filed through all the new dementia (If it hasn't already).

Mr.E @ 22:51 pst - Chris is OK! ^_^
Here's the good news:

First of all, sorry I need to keep this somewhat short and incomplete, but typing anything is kind of a strain right now. And while the effects of the drugs have just about faded completely, my prose may still seem a bit off.

Anyway, the short story is that everything went fine with the surgery on Wednesday morning. And I'll tell everybody the long story when I'm ready to update the site again.

You are the first person in the "outside world" that I'm contacting since returning home, and I am doing so because I wanted to express my gratitude for your support. I was absolutely stunned, and very moved, to see all of the responses on the message board. Your timing for the announcement on the front page (unexpected in itself) was perfect, and I was able to read it, as well as the first nine hours worth of responses, in the moments before I left for the hospital. It is important that I tell you that your gesture, and the kind words from your visitors, made that day and the whole experience overall, a lot easier than it might have been. I would have never dared expect a response like this.

In any case, if you decide to mention this on your site, please tell everyone not to expect me back at DBZ Uncensored for at least a week. It will take some time for my eye to heal, my body to return to full strength, and most importantly, for my sarcasm to return.

Rest assured though, I'll be back as soon as I can.

Thanks again!

This is absolutely outstanding to hear! That sure takes a load off my shoulders, and it's wonderful that the operation went well. I would like to personally thank all who wished Chris luck, for as you can see, it really did make a difference. We all hope he feels better, and that the operation on his other eye is just as successful. :)

Kaio @ 21:25 pst - More Updates!
I took some more time out of my busy schedule *COUGH* to make a bunch of small updates. I've added two more strategies to the card strategies page, added new chapters to two new fanfics in the fanfics section, replaced the Orion Saga with a different version (at the request of the author) in the fanmanga seciton, and subdued a grizzly bear using only a paper clip, rubber band, toothpick, and my teeth. In addition, thanks to a visitor submission (Planet Namek visitors rule!), the titles for all 104 songs on the Dragonball and Dragonball Z discs in the music vault are now present. Also, I made a little correction to the encyclopedia section, and was finally able to clean out my mailbox (YAY)!

AS OF 12:00 AM, EST, THE CONTEST IS CLOSED. Yes, the final submission has been sent, and the contest is now closed until the next one begins (perhaps in a month).

My computer is apparently trying to drive me insane, as it is now freezing up completely every fifty minutes (or less) regardless of what I'm doing. However, my new one will arrive on Monday, I am on the edge of my seat (not literally).

Mr.E @ 21:02 pst - Movie #8 Summary...
Kaio took some time out of his busy schedule *cough* to write a summary and review of DBZ Movie #8 for our movie guide. This is best known as Brolli's first appearance, and is by far the most plot-driven film of the thirteen (Quite a feat for a DBZ movie).

Mr.E @ 17:30 pst - Suncoast Release Date...Wrong?
I just got an e-mail from the webmaster at FUNimation's store...basically, it looks like Suncoast is pulling the BK Promo release date out of their asses:

I talked to the wholesale manager and he said that if Suncoast ships the BK video on the 3rd that they would be violating major contract agreements. He assured me that there is no way that Suncoast would be shipping the BK video on the third. Honestly, I would not be suprised if Suncoast ships the video on the 3rd regardless of what FUNimation says, that's what kind of dogs they are. Legally they should be shipped on the 19th. I like to help you guys out because the production studio neglects to tell us about a lot of stuff until I see it on your web site and ask them about it (i.e. release dates, the BK promo). If I hear anything from the wholesale man I'll let you know.

I knew that the shipping date was probably not the 3rd (as retailer dates are normally close, but not quite on the mark), and listed it as early September in the top news. Since Suncoast seems to be full of it, expect that tape more around mid-September.

Update: Ewww, a typo that slipped past me all day...I had written October instead of September...fixed.

Mr.E @ 15:28 pst - Sigh...
Alright, no no no, a thousand times NO. There is NO NEW DB SERIES IN JAPAN. Black Goku and others have posted a horribly blurry doujinshi cover of "Dragonball AF" where we see Goku in SSJ5 form (A doujinshi is basically a fanmanga in printed form. A lot of it is hentai). Toriyama and Toei have moved on, there will be nothing beyond GT. Don't wet yourselves over news with absolutely no sources, no concrete evidence, and no basis on fact. That is all.

Mr.E @ 13:18 pst - E-mail from Lois Gresh
A while back we had reported that a young boy and his mother had authored an unofficial guide to DBZ. Well, it seems Lois (the mother in the book) is paying attention to the web community, and sent me the following e-mail:

With my son, I'm the author of DRAGON BALL Z: AN UNAUTHORIZED GUIDE. The book was released a week ago. I've received more than 50 fan letters from kids aged 6-12 already. And I've received a pile of letters from people from the planet namek site (and other places, no doubt) just ranting about dubbies and pansy little kids and uncut Japanese episodes being the only DBZ shows worth seeing. Now, this is cool in a way, but...as you point out in the copyright statement on your site, the book is a fan book, we did not create DBZ, etc. We wrote the book simply to encourage people to watch the DBZ series. Same thing.

I can't keep posting replies to what I think of as the "uncut Japanese episode rant letters" because (1) I just don't have time for endless responses, and (2) I find these rant letters painful and sad. From all the fan letters I've received in just one week, I know that there are tons of kids watching the English-dubbed shows, reading the Viz comics, and playing with the action figures...just like me and Dan. We are fans, too. We perhaps cater to and promote a different fan base...the younger one that watches the shows on the cartoon network, etc. Even little kids have a right to watch anime, you know, and they really get off on their versions of dbz.

Luckily, I have a pretty thick skin, and I love cartoons, comics, and the whole sf-f genre enough to shrug off rants so I can keep dreaming and writing and playing. But I urge you guys to let up on the steam a little because you gotta think of the little kids coming up behind you...They have a write to be dbz fans, too, and maybe grow up to become writers of comicbooks and cartoons, and maybe artists who draw sf-f stuff as adults, and so forth. We all started somewhere, you know? Like the guy who posted on your site that he's a budding 13-year-old sf-f novelist...I say: go for it, man, you gotta start somewhere, and now's a great time to do it, when you're 13. I was writing wads of stuff, too, as a kid and teenager. We all start somewhere, usually as kids and teenagers, and then we just keep it at it and kid of never grow up. But if we beat on the kids who watch the cartoons on tv, and we beat on them enough that we pulverize them into dust, then who's gonna follow us to write cartoons and comics in 20 years? Or to become fans of the uncut stuff when they're bigger or when they finally can access the "international channel", which most people btw don't have...? see?

Fans are fans, and we all have different tastes and opinions; kind of the definition of fans, imo. This isn't a rant, btw. I applaud your planet namek site and every other dbz site, and god knows I've seen about a hundred of them now. (or more) Thank god there are so many fans of so many flavors, cause without them we wouldn't have cartoons or comics or fun movies or cool books, we'd just have boring, old junk, same old same old...and personally, I wouldn't be too happy or hold too much hope for us.

So, I like your site, and I'll come back again. Keep it up, and long live fandom of all kinds everywhere.

From a fan who never gave it up, Lois Gresh

Yes, there are younger fans, but they tend to stay away from the internet. Most people who come here are age 13 and up, so I think it's safe to discuss what is wrong with the dub. I'd like to apologize to Lois for all the spam she is getting about this book, and she really does have a point. While we might not see many 9 or 10 year-old fans on the internet, they still do exist, and they have been raised on the dub (Kind of like the Thundercats. Don't deny it...almost every young kid in the 80s watched that program, and I guarantee 95% did not know it was an anime).

Thursday - 08/17/2000

Mr.E @ 21:35 pst - New Hosted Site...
Yes, another one. ^_^ Rather than hosting especially crappy sites like many of the other networks, we here at PN feel that the original, yet somewhat neglected pages out there deserve some recognition. The Brian Drummond Mini-Shrine is a site that tells you all you need to know about B.A.D, who voiced Vegeta in seasons one and two of DBZ and Zechs in Gundam W. I know I say this every time, but WE ARE NOT ACCEPTING HOSTING REQUESTS. I physically go out and look for sites that I want to host, and submissions do not sway my favor anymore. Everyone go check the shrine out, it's one-of-a-kind.

Wednesday - 08/16/2000

Mr.E @ 11:44 pst - Little Updates...
Firstly and most importantly, I have added a little graphic to all characters, planets, sagas, and movies/specials that are "anime only" (You can see icon to the right). Basically, this is stuff that Toei added to the animated series that wasn't in the manga, or stuff from the movies. The FAQ has been updated with information on this defintion.

Secondly, I added the three androids from Movie #7 to our biographies (To all those who think the images of #13 and #15 are switched, shift-reload the page). Android #15 is one of my favorite character designs... ^_^

And lastly, I added some landscape shots of both of Old Namek's moons. I hope to add a shot to most of the other planets/moons in the near future.

Hopefully the anime/manga distinction will be more clear now; that was one big problem with our site that needed fixing.

Kaio @ 09:41 pst - New Card Strategies and Track List
I've updated the card game section to contain seven more strategies which were getting a bit stale in my mailbox.

Also, I've added the track list for the Dragonball Z "Never Ending Story" CD in the music vault. Enjoy the update and give a good word for Chris.

Mr.E @ 00:49 pst - Chris Psaros to Undergo Surgery...
Chris Psaros, or as most people know him, the head webmaster of DBZ Uncensored, is scheduled for complicated surgery tomorrow. As you can read in this post he made to alt.fan.dragonball, Chris was born with cataracts which have severly impaired his vision all his life. To sum it up, medical technology has greatly improved over the past two decades, and Chris is hoping that it will be able to repair his vision. While there is the possibility the operation could fail, there's still an excellent chance of success. Chris is who I look up to for guidance in the web community both as a person and a webmaster, and I've never heard a negative comment about him. He is, by far, the most respected DB fan on the internet. It's truely heart-breaking to hear what he has been going through for so many years. I would like to personally wish him the best of luck in his operation, and I hope it gives him a second chance at seeing clearly. Hang in there, Chris...I'm sure it helps to know that there are people out there who are wishing you success. ^_^

Mr.E @ 00:37 pst - Freddie Prinze Jr. Rumor Confirmed
Jon from DaizenshuuEX has received a brief e-mail from Gen Fukunaga that FUNimation is indeed negotiating with Freddie Prinze Jr.'s agent. However, his role is yet to be determined (We still have evidence that suggest Mirai Gohan is the most likely part he will get). Once again, they are trying to work something out, but nothing has been finalized yet. You can read the article here (Just to clarify: DaizenshuuEX and Namek are two different entities. EX has been complaining about slow people thinking he is part of our site).

Tuesday - 08/15/2000

Mr.E @ 21:47 pst - Z Warriors Prepare (BK Promo Tape) Available October 3rd...
Gotten two seperate parties to confirm this, but it looks like Suncoast will be shipping the Burger King promotion tape beginning October 3rd in both cut and uncut format ($7.99 and $9.99 respectively). Now, I mentioned this earlier with the Android release...but what the hell warrants a cut and uncut version of these tapes? There is absolutely, positively, NOTHING objectional in these two episodes (perhaps a "kuso," which they never translate to anything worse than "darn" anyway, even in the uncut version). My gut feeling is that FUNimation is cutting meaningless things from each episode just so they can sell an uncut version under the veil of being "fair." AND, we still are not certain these episodes will ever arrive in DVD format (though fans have been pushing for it).

Ah well...regardless, the stuff on this tape is some of my favorite DB material. It's sad to see it abused this way. Thanks to Shannon Lee and Frank Chapranda for the info!

Kaio @ 20:02 pst - Fan Manga 100%!
The fan manga section has been added. Currently, it contains only the Orion Saga, but I'm sure that we'll get more in the future. I can not wait to receive all the wonderful submissions that I am sure you will send and I will review and possibly upload to our wonderful site. *Looks nervously at Mr. E*

Update: At the request of Mr. E, CDC's fabulous Little Saiyalings strip has been moved to the fan manga section.

SkullMac @ 18:32 pst - Argggh! You paid $80 for WHAT?!?!
I've gotten enough e-mails about this to warrant a post. The DBGT Gameboy game floating around e-bay is a FAKE (Auction 1, Auction 2). Yes, I said a FAKE. Though I hate to see people get conned out of $80 (which is what the game sold for), I have NO pity for anyone who pays $80 for a "Taiwanese game" that has Japanese katakana on the cover of an American-style box! (See what I mean?) Come on people, THINK about what you're buying before you bid!

Mr.E @ 16:33 pst - Android Tapes Now Available...
It's the 15th, and the next two Android VHS tapes, Assassins and Invincible, are now widely available. *Points to mouth in gagging motion.* You know my thoughts on VHS. The DVDs will catch up to the tapes, so all I can say is sit on your hands and BE PATIENT. Cartoon Network will be airing the episodes shortly anyhow, and I can guarantee you that there's not much you will miss in the cut version of these episodes. It really makes me wonder why Uncut is more expensive when there's so little extra on each tape.

Update: Castor from Ginga GIRI GIRI has posted a couple clips from these new tapes. Check em out here.

SkullMac @ 12:43 pst - YTV Sets premier date for DBZ Season 4
Canadian DBZ fans rejoice, YTV has finally set a date for the premier of Dragonball Z. It will air at 7:00 PM on September 4th (the same day it premier in the US!). This is quite a step up for Canadian fans, as season 3 did not air in Canada until several months after the US premier. It should be noted that this is ONLY a premier date & time, and the regular air date & time is likely to change.

Source: Anime Nation

Kaio @ 09:51 pst - Fanfic Update!
The Fanfic Section was updated, and a good deal of new chapters have been added. Crono's Story received a rather large update, and an error in Twist of Fate was corrected. In addition, a fan manga section will be added later today, but I must upload the fan manga first. Anyway, enjoy the update. I think I will also do a card strategies update, as my mailbox is piling up with them.

Monday - 08/14/2000

Mr.E @ 20:31 pst - Lost 80's Dub...
Back in the 80's, a company named Harmony Gold dubbed the original Dragonball series into English. However, it did so poorly that the series was forgotten until FUNimation picked it up many years later. Meri from ToT has a new article dedicated to this lost batch of episodes, which has numerous sound clips and the theme song. You can have a look at this interesting moment in DB history here.

Mr.E @ 16:56 pst - Massive Universe Update...
Gather 'round boys and girls, and I shall tell you a tale... the universe section has received a galaxy-sized update (literally). The amount of heavenly bodies with info and images has more than doubled. The only concern of mine is planet placement...as of now, I can only assume that most systems are in the North Galaxy...though, more than likely, a few are in the other three. If you have any solid evidence that a planet is in the wrong place, tell me. ^_^ Anyhow, I plan to add the four missing systems in the near future, and a graphical representation of heaven. This is my favorite section to update, so I hope you all enjoyed it as much as I did!

Also: More IRC woes, it's under maitanence. Give us a week :/

Mr.E @ 11:21 pst - DBZ Game Article in Animerica...
Animerica has published an article in their latest issue that takes a look at the quality of DBZ Games on the market. Their assessment is fair, and they properly bash DB: Final Bout (come on, hitting someone when you are punching in the opposite direction?). I only wish they had reviewed some of the earlier SNES games (like Hyper Dimension) rather than going in-depth on just the PSX. Many, many, many kudos go to Meri of the Temple O' Trunks, who was nice enough to scan this for us. ^_^ The article is in JPG format, and is available here.

Saturday - 08/12/2000

Mr.E @ 16:02 pst - Poll Time...
For some reason our last poll got reset a few days ago...however, there was still plenty of votes to see that about half of you either have or will buy the Ginyu: Assault DVD. Let's hope the same thing applies to Double Cross. Our new poll is simple...and EVERYONE needs to vote on this one. Although we know already what the results will be, we can show Viz that they will be losing customers by censoring the comic. Cast your vote, it's important...

Mr.E @ 13:11 pst - DB DVDs...
Kidmark has released the first few episodes of the original Dragonball on DVD. Don't get excited yet...they are dub only (heavily edited as well). It is doubtful we will see sub-titled discs of these episodes, just like the ones Pioneer owns. And yes, I *know* there are DVDs for seasons one and two...BUT, once again, they only have the dub. Pioneer is the only company that can release a sub-titled version, so it's no use bitching at FUNimation. There is always the possibility a sub-titled dvd will be released, but don't count on it.

Mr.E @ 03:18 pst - Wheeeee...
That's right, it's 3 am...too damn late for me to be working on the site. -_- However, my fanart inbox was piling up, and it was time for a massive update. This particular update features two whole pages of outstanding images...so much good stuff came in all at once. It was such a battle for the title image this time...but Joe Tubby won the spot with his spiffy drawing of Goku as Forrest Gump (Not much of a stretch, but still original ^_^). However, fanart submissions are TEMPORARILY CLOSED. The next two weeks are completely hectic for me, and I really can't take another pile-up of images until I'm back in the normal loop.

I'll meet you all in the sand man's domain...g'night everyone!

Mr.E @ 00:19 pst - GenCon Report...
Some lucky individuals out there are going to a convention...while I sit here at home and drool on my unfinished homework :( Here's a take on the Con from David Leubner:

Now after standing in line for abour a half hour, finally I got to meet Sean and Sonny. Sonny didn't say much to me, but me and Sean talked about movie #12 because thats what my poster was about. Anyhow, he said they're talking about dubbing it.

So at 4:00 Sean and Sonny Switched with Meredith and Chris, and I got to get their autograph too. Me nad my friend where first in line for that ^_^ It's too bad their isn't an Android #18 card, well she signed my poster also. Then Chris drew Krillen on my poster, and wrote "HOW COME I'M NOT ON YOUR POSTER?!" He also drew Android #18 on my friends poster. I told him he should be a manga artist, and he said he's working on elf quest! stupid me.

Anyway, now my poster is nicely up on my wall with their 4 autographs, and I have cards signed also. I even heard them swear in their characters voices. It was beautiful, and so was Meredith (hee!) My friend's brother also got his signature on a card from Chris that said "KAME HAME HOLY ##!?" Yes, it was all fun and games! Dragonball Z is big at Gen Con this year also, so I would suggest that everyone go there.

Sounds like the VAs are having fun, as usual. It's no news that movie #12 will be dubbed...it's a matter of when. I doubt we'll see it for at least another two years.

Friday - 08/11/2000

Mr.E @ 20:17 pst - Biographies Update...
For the past two days I have been busy scanning and writing content for our biographies section. Here's all the changes/additions: About half the races have been added to our race list, more characters, more techniques, lots of portraits were replaced, many bios were rewritten, and screenshots have been added to some characters. No, the section is not done...but, it is getting there. I didn't put images for all the new stuff, but in the future, portraits will be added. Enjoy everyone...

Mr.E @ 13:10 pst - Special Guide Update...
Thanks to Jon Morey (Ridureyu), the synopsises/reviews of both DBZ specials have been completed. As far as DBZ goes, that section is complete...however, there IS one TV special in DBGT that I have not found a movie poster for. If you have it, please send it in. You can check out the update here.


Previous News

Disclaimers
Planet Namek is just a fansite, we did NOT create Dragonball, nor did we have any part in the production process.

©2000, Bird Studio/Shueisha, Toei Animation. Licensed by FUNimation Productions, Inc. All Rights Reserved, or as otherwise directed by FUNimation. This site is not meant to infringe on any copyrights, it is simply here to encourage people to watch the Dragonball series.

Although we did not create the series, we did take a lot of time to work on this site and bring you original files and information. Please honor our efforts by not stealing our files/layout from us. Thank you.

Web Page Design © Planet Namek

Best viewed in 800 by 600 resolution or better & thousands of colors or more.
For the best experience, set your browser to view images "better" rather than "faster."
